howItWorks.intro: This canada employment insurance calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Employee premium = Insurable earnings x 1.66% (1.312% in Quebec), max $63,200
Weekly benefit = 55% of average weekly insurable earnings, max $668/week
Benefit weeks vary by hours worked and regional unemployment rate
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates. Consult a professional for critical decisions.

faq.question: What is the 2024 EI premium rate?
faq.answer: The 2024 employee EI premium rate is $1.66 per $100 of insurable earnings (1.66%), or $1.312 in Quebec. The employer pays 1.4 times the employee rate. Maximum insurable earnings are $63,200, so the maximum annual employee premium is $1,049.12.

faq.question: How much will I receive on EI?
faq.answer: EI pays 55% of your average insurable weekly earnings, up to a maximum of $668 per week in 2024. The number of weeks depends on hours worked and the regional unemployment rate.

faq.question: How many hours do I need to qualify for EI?
faq.answer: You generally need 420-700 insurable hours in the past 52 weeks, depending on your region's unemployment rate. Areas with higher unemployment require fewer hours to qualify.

faq.question: Can self-employed people get EI?
faq.answer: Self-employed individuals can opt into EI special benefits (maternity, parental, sickness, compassionate care) but not regular benefits. You pay the employee premium but the employer portion is waived.

sections.title: What Is the Canada Employment Insurance Calculator?
sections.body: The Canada Employment Insurance Calculator helps you get precise numbers for your specific situation in seconds. Instead of guessing or spending hours on manual calculations, get accurate results in seconds. Enter your details above and let the calculator do the work.

sections.title: Understanding the Canadian System
sections.body: Canadian financial calculations involve federal and provincial components that interact in ways that are easy to miscalculate manually. This tool accounts for those interactions so you get an accurate picture of your real obligations and benefits.
